name: Bank API
description: Minimalist modular-monolith banking REST API — IAM, double-entry accounts, transfers with idempotency, loans, append-only audit, and async notifications. Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL, Upstash Redis, and Kafka consumed from a cloud broker; observability stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki) runs on EC2.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Learning banking domains without microservice overhead
problemStatement.problemDescription: Building a credible banking backend requires double-entry ledgers, idempotent payments, loan amortization, audit trails, and secure auth — but a full microservice mesh is heavy for a portfolio or teaching project. Teams also need a path from local Docker to a real cloud deploy without rewriting the domain model.
problemList: Monolithic tutorials skip idempotency, ledger integrity, and append-only audit
JWT + RBAC + refresh rotation is often bolted on without Redis-backed revocation
Cross-module side effects (transfers → ledger → notifications) are hard to test in isolation
Local dev stacks differ from production (H2 vs PostgreSQL, in-process events vs Kafka)
Observability (metrics, structured logs, audit files) is rarely wired end-to-end

solution.solutionTitle: Modular monolith with clean boundaries and AWS-ready deploy

solutionList.title: Domain modules with hexagonal layering
solutionList.description: Nine Maven modules (shared, iam, accounts, payments, loans, audit, notifications, config, boot) — controllers stay thin; handlers own use cases; domain has zero Spring imports.

solutionList.title: Financial correctness by design
solutionList.description: Balances derived from ledger entries (never stored), BigDecimal money, transfer state machine, loan amortization schedule, and Result<T> for domain failures.

solutionList.title: Production integrations enabled in docker profile
solutionList.description: Redis for refresh tokens, JWT blocklist, payment idempotency, and token-bucket rate limits; Kafka ingress for notification dispatch when BANK_KAFKA_ENABLED=true.

solutionList.title: Deployed minimalist stack on AWS
solutionList.description: Single EC2 instance runs the app container (docker/compose.yml); RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis are external; Kafka broker is a cloud instance the app consumes from; Prometheus/Grafana/Loki for observability on EC2.

solutionList.title: OpenAPI-first API surface
solutionList.description: REST under /api/v1/* with Swagger UI at /swagger-ui.html and standardized ApiResponse envelope.

metricsList: REST API v1 under /api/v1/ (auth, accounts, payments, loans, audit, notifications)
Docker image: Eclipse Temurin 21 JRE Alpine, ZGC, healthcheck on /actuator/health
Local full stack: postgres, redis, kafka, prometheus, grafana, loki, nginx via compose.local.yml

mediaGallery.title: Bank API — product views
mediaGallery.description: Screenshots and diagrams for portfolio presentation. Replace placeholder URLs with Swagger UI, Grafana dashboard, or architecture exports from your AWS deploy.

metrics.label: Maven modules
metrics.description: shared, iam, accounts, audit, payments, loans, notifications, config, boot

metrics.label: Java runtime
metrics.description: Eclipse Temurin JDK/JRE in Docker multi-stage build

metrics.label: Auth

layers.name: Presentation (API)
layers.description: REST controllers per domain module — no business logic, only DTO mapping and handler delegation.

layers.name: Application layer
layers.description: Command/query handlers orchestrate use cases, publish domain events, and coordinate transactions.

layers.name: Domain layer
layers.description: Pure Java aggregates, value objects, and port interfaces — zero Spring/JPA dependencies.
components: Transfer state machine, Loan amortization rules
responsibilities: Enforce invariants (balance from ledger, transfer states)

layers.name: Infrastructure layer
layers.description: JPA adapters, Redis stores, Kafka producers/consumers, and external notification stubs.
responsibilities: Bridge to RDS, Upstash Redis, cloud Kafka

layers.name: Data & messaging (AWS)
layers.description: External managed services — not bundled in production compose.
components: Cloud Kafka broker (notification dispatch + pipeline topics)
responsibilities: Shared state across EC2 app restarts
Decouple notification enqueue from HTTP thread

layers.name: Observability (EC2)
layers.description: External monitoring stack running alongside or on the same EC2 host.

designPatterns.title: Modular monolith
designPatterns.description: Nine Maven modules with package-level boundaries; any module extractable to microservice by swapping ApplicationEvent for Kafka/SQS.

designPatterns.title: Hexagonal (ports & adapters)
designPatterns.description: domain/port.out interfaces implemented in infrastructure/persistence/adapter — domain never imports JPA.

designPatterns.title: CQRS-lite
designPatterns.description: Separate command and query handlers with dedicated Command/Query records.

designPatterns.title: Domain events
designPatterns.description: TransferCompletedEvent, LoanDisbursedEvent, etc. in bank-shared; listeners in accounts, audit, notifications.

designPatterns.title: Result<T> for failures
designPatterns.description: Expected domain failures (INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS) return Result.failure instead of exceptions for control flow.

designPatterns.title: Idempotency key
designPatterns.description: Redis + DB dedup for payment mutations — safe retries from mobile/unstable networks.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API container
scalabilityStrategies.description: JWT + external Redis — scale horizontally with additional EC2 instances behind ALB; shared Upstash Redis for tokens and idempotency.

scalabilityStrategies.title: RDS PostgreSQL
scalabilityStrategies.description: Managed backups and storage; connection pooling via HikariCP; Flyway migrations on deploy.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Kafka notification pipeline
scalabilityStrategies.description: Decouple notification dispatch from request thread; consumer group on cloud Kafka handles burst traffic.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Rate limiting fail-open
scalabilityStrategies.description: Redis token bucket protects auth and payments; configurable fail-open if Redis unreachable (logged, not silent).

securityStrategies.title: RS256 JWT + permissions
securityStrategies.description: Spring Security filter chain; @PreAuthorize on sensitive endpoints; permissions[] in JWT claims.

securityStrategies.title: Refresh rotation + blocklist
securityStrategies.description: Old refresh invalidated on rotate; logout adds jti to Redis blocklist for remaining access token TTL.

securityStrategies.title: Append-only audit
securityStrategies.description: DB trigger prevents audit record mutation; full event JSON in payload for reconstruction.

securityStrategies.title: Idempotency on writes
securityStrategies.description: Payments require Idempotency-Key; prevents duplicate transfers on client retries.

securityStrategies.title: CORS configuration
securityStrategies.description: bank.http.cors.allowed-origins from env — empty by default; set explicitly for frontends.

cacheStrategies.name: IAM refresh tokens
cacheStrategies.description: Redis key iam:refresh:{sha256(token)} → userId, TTL 7 days

cacheStrategies.name: JWT blocklist
cacheStrategies.description: Redis key blocklist:{jti} for revoked access tokens

cacheStrategies.name: Rate limit buckets
cacheStrategies.description: Lua token-bucket script — atomic refill + consume per IP/user key

architectureFeatures.title: Spring Modulith
architectureFeatures.description: Formal module boundaries with event-based integration; supports future extraction without package rewrites.

architectureFeatures.title: Flyway migrations
architectureFeatures.description: 15 versioned SQL migrations + repeatable seed; ddl-auto=validate in production.

architectureFeatures.title: Standard ApiResponse
architectureFeatures.description: Uniform { data, meta, errors } JSON for all REST endpoints.

architectureFeatures.title: Actuator observability
architectureFeatures.description: health, info, prometheus endpoints exposed per MANAGEMENT_ENDPOINTS_WEB_EXPOSURE_INCLUDE.

requestFlow.title: HTTP request
requestFlow.description: Client sends REST call with Bearer JWT (except public auth routes).

requestFlow.title: Security + rate limit
requestFlow.description: JwtAuthenticationFilter validates RS256 token; GlobalRateLimitFilter and @RateLimit check Redis token bucket.

requestFlow.title: Handler execution
requestFlow.description: Controller delegates to application handler; domain rules enforced; @Transactional wraps persistence.

requestFlow.title: Persistence & cache
requestFlow.description: JPA writes to RDS; Redis updated for idempotency or rate counters.

requestFlow.title: Events & response
requestFlow.description: AFTER_COMMIT listeners update ledger, audit, notifications; ApiResponse JSON returned.

eventFlow.title: Domain event published
eventFlow.description: e.g. TransferCompletedEvent after successful transfer commit.

eventFlow.title: Cross-module listeners
eventFlow.description: accounts posts ledger entries; audit appends AuditRecord; notifications builds DispatchNotificationCommand.

eventFlow.title: Kafka enqueue (AWS)
eventFlow.description: When dispatch-mode=kafka, KafkaNotificationDispatchIngress publishes to bank.notifications.dispatch topic on cloud broker.

eventFlow.title: Notification delivery
eventFlow.description: Kafka consumer processes pipeline; email/SMS sent or stubbed; status logged in notification_log table.

techDecisions.title: Modular monolith vs microservices
techDecisions.problem: Portfolio project needs credible domain boundaries without Kubernetes operational cost.
techDecisions.solution: Maven multi-module monolith with ApplicationEvent + optional Kafka; single Docker image on EC2.
techDecisions.outcome: Fast iteration locally; clean extraction path documented in .agents/architecture.md.

techDecisions.title: PostgreSQL on RDS
techDecisions.problem: Financial data requires ACID, relational integrity, and append-only audit with triggers.
techDecisions.solution: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL 16; Flyway migrations; Hibernate ddl-auto=validate.
techDecisions.outcome: Production-grade persistence; same SQL dialect in local compose.local.yml.

techDecisions.title: Upstash Redis
techDecisions.problem: JWT refresh, blocklist, idempotency, and rate limits need shared state across EC2 restarts.
techDecisions.solution: External Upstash Redis — not containerized in production compose.yml.
techDecisions.outcome: Survives container redeploys; works with multiple EC2 instances behind ALB.

techDecisions.title: Cloud Kafka for notifications
techDecisions.problem: Notification dispatch should not block HTTP threads or be lost on app restart.
techDecisions.solution: Kafka broker on cloud instance; BANK_KAFKA_ENABLED=true and dispatch-mode=kafka in docker profile.
techDecisions.outcome: Same code path testable locally via compose.local.yml kafka service.

techDecisions.title: EC2 observability stack
techDecisions.problem: Need metrics and logs without managed CloudWatch-only setup for portfolio demo.
techDecisions.solution: Prometheus scrapes app on EC2; Grafana dashboards; Loki for log aggregation; optional ELK profile.
techDecisions.outcome: Full visibility documented in docker/MONITORING.md and docs/OBSERVABILITY.md.

codeExamples.title: Standardized API response envelope
codeExamples.description: All REST endpoints return ApiResponse<T> with data, meta.timestamp, and errors[] for logical failures.

files.name: ApiResponse.java
files.explanation: Success and failure factories keep controller responses consistent across modules.

codeExamples.title: Transfer initiation with idempotency
codeExamples.description: Payments check Redis cache and DB before processing — duplicate Idempotency-Key returns cached Result.

files.name: InitiateTransferHandler.java
files.explanation: Idempotency-Key header is required; self-transfer and currency rules enforced before state transition.

codeExamples.title: Redis token-bucket rate limiter
codeExamples.description: Atomic Lua script refill + consume in one round trip; fail-open when Redis errors if configured.

files.name: RedisTokenBucketRateLimiter.java
files.explanation: Used by GlobalRateLimitFilter and AnnotatedRateLimitInterceptor in docker/AWS profile.

codeExamples.title: Kafka notification dispatch ingress
codeExamples.description: When dispatch-mode=kafka, notifications are enqueued to cloud broker instead of processed inline.

files.name: KafkaNotificationDispatchIngress.java
files.explanation: Active only when bank.notifications.dispatch-mode=kafka — matches AWS deploy configuration.

codeExamples.title: Money value object
codeExamples.description: Immutable currency-aware amount with BigDecimal scale 2 — used everywhere financial math is needed.

files.name: Money.java
files.explanation: Compact constructor rejects negative amounts and normalizes scale — never use double for money.

features.title: JWT authentication & RBAC
features.description: RS256 JWT with roles (CUSTOMER, ADMIN, AUDITOR) and fine-grained permissions. Refresh tokens stored in Upstash Redis with rotation on refresh; jti blocklist on logout.
highlights: Access TTL 15 min, refresh TTL 7 days
Redis-backed refresh store enabled in docker/AWS profile

features.title: Double-entry ledger accounts
features.description: CHECKING, SAVINGS, and LOAN accounts. Balance always computed from ledger entries — never stored as a mutable field.
highlights: Ledger entries on transfers, loan disbursement, repayments

features.title: Transfers with idempotency
features.description: PENDING → PROCESSING → COMPLETED state machine. Redis idempotency cache (24h) plus DB dedup. Publishes TransferCompletedEvent for ledger and notifications.
highlights: Result<T> pattern for domain errors → HTTP 422

features.title: Loan origination & amortization
features.description: Fixed monthly payment schedule at origination. Approval creates LOAN account and disburses to checking. Repayments debit checking via domain events.

features.title: Append-only audit trail
features.description: Every BankDomainEvent captured as immutable AuditRecord (JSONB payload). DB trigger prevents UPDATE/DELETE.
highlights: AuditEventListener subscribes to all domain events
UUID v7 time-ordered IDs for range queries

features.title: Async notifications (direct + Kafka)
features.description: Fire-and-forget email/SMS on transfer and loan events. In AWS/docker profile, dispatch-mode=kafka enqueues to cloud Kafka; consumer in bank-notifications processes pipeline.
highlights: Dev stubs log to console; production uses SMTP/SMS config

features.title: Redis token-bucket rate limiting
features.description: Global filter + @RateLimit annotation on controllers. Lua script for atomic refill/consume. Fail-open on Redis errors when configured.

features.title: OpenAPI & standardized responses
features.description: springdoc-openapi with @BankApiOperation annotations. ApiResponse<T> envelope with data, meta.timestamp, and errors[].

features.title: Docker + AWS production deploy
features.description: Multi-stage Temurin 21 JRE image (~300MB). compose.yml runs app only on EC2; RDS, Upstash Redis, and cloud Kafka via .env. Observability on EC2.
highlights: docker/compose.yml — single app container on EC2

metrics.label: Container port
metrics.description: Spring Boot embedded Tomcat; exposed in Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK

metrics.label: Host mapping (deploy)
metrics.description: docker/compose.yml publishes API port on EC2 (security group)

metrics.label: Image size target
metrics.description: Multi-stage build — JRE + single fat JAR only in runtime stage

cloudServices.name: Amazon EC2
cloudServices.purpose: Runs bank-api Docker container (compose.yml). Optional Nginx reverse proxy. Hosts Prometheus, Grafana, Loki for external observability.

cloudServices.name: Amazon RDS (PostgreSQL 16)
cloudServices.purpose: Primary database — IAM, accounts, ledger, transfers, loans, audit, notifications. SPRING_DATASOURCE_* in .env.

cloudServices.name: Upstash Redis
cloudServices.purpose: JWT refresh tokens, jti blocklist, transfer idempotency cache, token-bucket rate limits. SPRING_DATA_REDIS_* in .env.

cloudServices.name: Cloud Kafka instance
cloudServices.purpose: Notification dispatch and pipeline topics (bank.notifications.dispatch, bank.notifications.pipeline). App produces and consumes via SPRING_KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS.

cloudServices.name: Prometheus (on EC2)
cloudServices.purpose: Scrapes /actuator/prometheus from app. PROMETHEUS_SCRAPE_TARGET points to app host:port.

cloudServices.name: Grafana + Loki (on EC2)
cloudServices.purpose: Dashboards and log aggregation. GRAFANA_PROMETHEUS_URL and LOKI_URL configured per deploy.

cloudServices.name: SMTP / SMS (optional)
cloudServices.purpose: Real notification delivery when not using dev stubs. BANK_NOTIFICATIONS_DEV_REDIRECT_EMAIL for safe testing.

components.name: REST clients
components.description: Postman, curl, or future web/mobile frontend consuming /api/v1/*

components.name: Nginx (optional)
components.description: TLS termination and reverse proxy to app:8080 (local: compose.local.yml nginx on :80)

components.name: Docker — bank-api app
components.description: Built from docker/Dockerfile; SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=docker; restart unless-stopped

components.name: Observability agents
components.description: Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Promtail on EC2 (or same compose.local.yml stack locally)

components.name: RDS PostgreSQL
components.description: External — not in production compose.yml. Flyway applies schema on startup.

components.name: Upstash Redis
components.description: External TLS Redis — tokens, idempotency, rate limits

components.name: Cloud Kafka
components.description: External broker — notification dispatch consumed by bank-notifications module

components.name: App logs volume
components.description: BANK_LOGGING_DIRECTORY=/app/logs — audit.json, access.json; Promtail ships to Loki

components.name: Containerized Postgres
components.description: postgres:16-alpine for dev — not used in AWS production deploy

components.name: Containerized Redis + Kafka
components.description: Local kafka:9092 and redis for parity testing before cloud cutover

components.name: Optional ELK profile
components.description: docker compose --profile elk — Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana

dockerFiles.description: Single app service — Postgres, Redis, and Kafka are external cloud instances configured via repo-root .env.
dockerFiles.content:
name: bank-api
services:
  app:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile
    image: bank-api:${BANK_API_IMAGE_TAG}
    env_file:
      - ../.env
    environment:
      SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: docker
    ports:
      - "${APP_HTTP_PORT}:8080"
    restart: unless-stopped
# External: RDS PostgreSQL + Upstash Redis + Cloud Kafka
# Observability: Prometheus/Grafana/Loki on EC2 (see docker/MONITORING.md)

dockerFiles.description: JDK 21 Alpine builder runs mvn package; JRE 21 Alpine runtime runs as non-root bank user.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine AS builder
RUN ./mvnw -pl bank-boot -am package -DskipTests
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine
USER bank
EXPOSE 8080
ENV JAVA_OPTS="-XX:+UseZGC"
HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8080/actuator/health
ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "exec java $JAVA_OPTS -jar /app/app.jar"]

dockerFiles.description: postgres, redis, kafka, prometheus, grafana, loki, promtail, nginx, app — for local integration testing.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  postgres:   # postgres:16-alpine
  redis:      # redis:7-alpine
  kafka:      # confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.5.0
  prometheus: # scrapes app:8080/actuator/prometheus
  grafana:    # dashboards + Loki datasource
  loki:       # log aggregation
  app:        # bank-api Spring Boot
  nginx:      # :80 → app:8080

httpEndpoints.description: Spring Boot Actuator liveness/readiness probe for load balancers, Docker HEALTHCHECK, and EC2 monitoring.
Micrometer metrics in Prometheus text format. Scraped by Prometheus on EC2 (see docker/MONITORING.md).
Interactive OpenAPI documentation for all registered endpoints.
Creates a customer account and returns JWT access + refresh tokens.
Email/password login. Returns access and refresh tokens (RS256 JWT with roles and permissions claims).
Issues new access + refresh tokens; old refresh token invalidated in Redis.
Requires Bearer access token. Adds jti to Redis blocklist for remaining TTL.
Returns userId and username for the authenticated principal.
Creates CHECKING, SAVINGS, or LOAN account for the authenticated user.
Balance derived from ledger SUM(CREDIT) - SUM(DEBIT). User must own the account.
Returns DEBIT/CREDIT entries for the account (default page size 20).
Moves funds between two accounts owned by the user. Requires Idempotency-Key header (UUID). Same-currency only; no FX in v1.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Client-generated idempotency key (24h Redis TTL in docker profile)

responses.description: Domain failure (insufficient funds, currency mismatch, etc.)

httpEndpoints.description: Only COMPLETED transfers may be reversed. Requires Idempotency-Key header.
Creates a loan in PENDING_APPROVAL with amortization schedule generated at origination.
Transitions loan to ACTIVE, creates LOAN account, credits checking account via domain events.
Returns loan aggregate including repayment schedule and status.
Debits customer checking account; marks repayment PAID; may transition loan to PAID_OFF.
Append-only audit log query. Requires audit:read permission. Filters by eventType, actorId, entityType, entityId, date range.
Operational monitoring of sent/failed notifications. Filter by status and channel.
Aggregate counts by status and channel for ops dashboards.
